msgs.text:
According to the attached document from 2026-06-28, Turn 138 is another attachment-only continuation. The three required current-turn files are present, and I used only mega_prompt.md, problem_statement.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md.

Turn 138 — attachment-only idempotence remains closed

The state remains unchanged from Turn 137:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).The problem remains exactly the over-G isomorphism question: whether there exists a continuous isomorphismΦ:ΓS→ΓH such thatπH∘Φ=πS,with both groups carrying the canonical quotient maps to G.The theorem coordinate remains closed. The attached sweep records the stronger F=P⋊G-level statement:∃Φ:F∼

F over Gsuch thatΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this gives Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,and therefore descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,i.e.ΓS≅GΓH.

This is exactly the requested condition πH∘Φ=πS.No structural subcase should be reopened under the present inputs. The frozen proof chain remains the uniform one:H1 -Nielsen equivalence+ residual restricted-Lie induction+ normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+ Certificates D.1–D.4+ finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The attached history identifies the structural reason: non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled; the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower closes by Cm=Lm; the Ef-coefficient tower closes after exact normalization of the local row to (2,t) and the Koszul–Witt cascade; and finite filtered Nielsen compactness globalizes the finite filtered solutions.The compactness mechanism remains the corrected filtered-Nielsen one. For coefficient-and-degree truncations PN,J, the finite sets SN,J consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary quotient automorphisms; the finite data have invertible first Magnus layer, give finite over-G automorphisms, and their compatible inverse limit gives Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH.The strict measured Certificate D coordinate also remains unchanged. The current attachments are still only the prompt/problem/history set; they do not include genuine object-level nonlinear Certificate D.1–D.4 artifacts such as independent scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs. Therefore the strict measured gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. Ledger-recorded verifier claims support the theorem coordinate, but they are not newly attached measured artifacts for the strict Certificate D gate.

Thus Turn 138 is idempotent:{mega_prompt.md,problem_statement.md,turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md}⟹(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

ESTABLISHED: Turn 138 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 137. The theorem coordinate remains ΓS≅GΓH, via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN138-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
